narrative: Dear [Student's Name], as a Black woman applying to law school, you've poured your heart into this journey, and I know receiving this rejection must feel incredibly disheartening amid the unique challenges you may have faced. The admissions model evaluated your profile— including your undergraduate GPA of 2.80, LSAT score of 38, Year 1 and Year 3 school deciles of 3 and 2 respectively, upper-middle family income, and plan to attend full-time—against patterns from thousands of past students' bar exam outcomes. It predicted a 74.7% chance that you would fail the bar exam on your first try, which is why your application was rejected; the school uses this to prioritize applicants likely to succeed and pass the bar promptly. The most influential factor pushing toward this failure prediction was your Year 3 decile ranking of 2, well below the typical student's 5.53 average, signaling to the model a notable dip in later academic performance compared to peers. Your Year 1 decile of 3, also lower than the average of 5.71, reinforced this concern as the next biggest influence in the same direction. On a brighter note, your LSAT score of 38—stronger than the average of 36.70—worked significantly in your favor, pulling the prediction toward bar passage success. Your undergraduate GPA of 2.80, slightly below the typical 3.21, added a modest push toward the failure risk, while your upper-middle family income and full-time enrollment plans offered small positive boosts, though not enough to outweigh the top concerns. Overall, your profile stands out from typical admitted students, who tend to have higher decile rankings in school grades, making those early academic indicators the key hurdles here. This isn't a judgment on your potential or the barriers you've overcome—it's just the model's data-driven lens on bar exam patterns. To move forward, consider retaking the LSAT to build on your already solid score, strengthening your later-year grades through targeted coursework or post-bac programs, and exploring schools with holistic reviews that value your background. You're resilient, and with focused improvements on those academic deciles, you can reshape this story and secure the path you deserve. Keep pushing—you've got this.
